meta.title: Kutchan Asahigaoka Ski Area — LP Mock (Local Hill · Dual Pricing Strategy)
meta.description: Niseko's hidden local hill. About 3 minutes from Kutchan Station, no lift lines, Mt. Yotei views. LP mock based on Kutchan Visitor Pass, delivery rental, and Yukinko Hall night bar strategy.

banner.text: Local hill · dual pricing · Kutchan Visitor Pass strategy | AI-generated mock images

hero.title:
Asahigaoka
Ski Area
hero.tagline: Niseko's hidden local hill—without the lift lines.

transit.title:
A quiet hill
right beside the mega-resorts.
transit.lead: One step away from Hirafu and Annupuri crowds and prices—about 3 minutes by car from Kutchan Station and downtown. One pair lift, two courses over 560 m—a local sanctuary for visitors who "came to Niseko but couldn't ski" and for resident families alike.
transit.imgAlt: A quiet local slope with Mt. Yotei in the background—just outside Niseko's bustle

primary.title: Mt. Yotei views on a local hill
primary.lead: A compact municipal hill with Mt. Yotei in sight from every run. Two courses over 560 m, max pitch 30°—inside Niseko's snow-quality zone, but free from queues and crowds: "Niseko's backyard."
primary.imgAlt: Mt. Yotei from Asahigaoka slopes—a clear-day panorama

secondary.title: A sled zone built for safety
secondary.lead: A dedicated sled course physically separated from ski slopes. Young children stay out of the skiing area by design—the differentiator behind "Niseko's most family-friendly local ski hill."

pass.title:
Local ¥1,500,
Visitor ¥4,500.
pass.lead: Keep the resident day ticket at ¥1,500 while offering inbound and resort guests the Kutchan Visitor Pass at ¥4,500—a strategic dual-pricing model against Niseko-area rates (LP concept).
pass.noticeBody: Protect "everyday local pricing" against rising resort costs nearby, and lift ARPU through the Visitor Pass—a revenue-model pivot for a local hill.
pass.imgAlt: About 3 minutes from downtown Kutchan—easy access that sustains local use

rental.title:
Boom Sports
delivers to your stay.
rental.lead: A delivery rental pack in partnership with Boom Sports in Kutchan. Skis, snowboards, and outerwear shipped to your accommodation beforehand—resort guests without a car can head straight to Asahigaoka.
rental.whisper: Reduce lodge hand-out load and route Niseko-area "hands-free arrival" demand to Asahigaoka—an external synergy play for revenue growth.
rental.imgAlt: Boom Sports rental gear—delivery to your accommodation

yukinko.title:
Yukinko Hall,
night snow bar.
yukinko.lead: Food, drinks, and rest at Yukinko Hall (雪ん子館) at the base. A night snow bar aligned with Tue/Thu night skiing until 21:00—secondary spend and longer stays after runs.
yukinko.whisper: Winter TEL 0136-23-2743 (Yukinko Hall). From lodge meals to "Asahigaoka nights"—an F&B hub that feels local.
yukinko.imgAlt: Warm lodge space at Yukinko Hall—a night snow bar concept

family.title:
Niseko's most
family-friendly local hill.
family.lead: Dedicated sled zone, two compact courses, no lift lines—a low-stress full day with small children. The everyday hill for local families, open to inbound family travelers too.
family.noticeBody: Licensed schools limited to 2–3 concessionaires—avoid oversupply, maintain quality and safety standards for family lessons (strategy proposal).
family.imgAlt: Families in the sled zone—physically separated from ski slopes

workation.title:
Remote AM,
3 hours on snow PM.
workation.lead: About 3 minutes by car from Kutchan coworking spaces and lodging—a short-time pass journey for workation guests who want both desk time and runs.

01.title: Kutchan base
01.body: Remote work AM at downtown lodging or coworking

02.title: Delivery rental
02.body: Boom Sports ships gear to your accommodation in advance

03.title: Asahigaoka short pass
03.body: 3-hour ticket for efficient laps on two compact courses

04.title: Yukinko Hall
04.body: F&B break. Tue/Thu you can extend into night skiing

05.title: Back to town
05.body: ~3 min to central Kutchan—dinner and onsen

local.title: Complete guide to Niseko's hidden local hill
local.body: Inside Hirafu and Annupuri's crowded, premium-priced zone—yet ~3 minutes from Kutchan and virtually no lift wait. One pair lift, two courses over 560 m, max 30°—Asahigaoka's pitch: mega-resort snow quality, local-hill calm.

schools.title: Concession schools & safety guide
schools.body: Licensed schools capped at 2–3 concessionaires—prevent oversupply, protect family-lesson quality. Covers physical separation of sled and ski zones and Tue/Thu night skiing (until 21:00) operating rules.

summer.title: Asahigaoka in summer—shibazakura & camping
summer.body: Off-season monetization via shibazakura viewing and campground operations, referenced in guide features only. Seasonal diversification beyond the ski hill—Kutchan as a year-round outdoor base (LP strategy note).

access.line: 北海道虻田郡倶知安町字旭37番地1
access.note: About 3 minutes by car from Kutchan Station and downtown · ~100 parking spaces
